role: Founder of the Worker-communist Party of Iran — Hekmatist; Marxist politician
bio: Koorosh Modarresi is an Iranian Marxist politician and one of the leading figures in the Worker-communist tradition following the death of its founder [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat) in 2002. After Hekmat's death, a factional dispute erupted within the [Worker-communist Party of Iran (WPI)](/orgs/worker-communist-party-of-iran) over leadership and political direction. Modarresi led a faction that split from the WPI in 2004 to form the [Hekmatist Party](/orgs/hekmatist-party) (formally the Worker-communist Party of Iran — Hekmatist), claiming to uphold Hekmat's legacy more faithfully. The party is based in exile and focuses on socialist politics and secularism in opposition to the Islamic Republic. Modarresi remains active as a political commentator on Iranian leftist and labour politics.
editorial_notes: Led 2004 split from WPI to form Hekmatist Party. Based in exile. Active political commentator.
